summary:
Each week, the instructor or the students will select an audio/video recording, such as a YouTube video or radio story, as a basis for English study, class discussion and presentations. Initially, the instructor will select recordings based on students' ability and interests. Later, after students have become familiar with various Internet resources for English study, they will choose materials on their own.
Students will summarize the content of each recording, present their summaries and thoughts, and discuss the content with their classmates. Through this format, students will acquire English vocabulary and subject knowledge to talk about topics that interest them, improve their conversational fluency and attain skills for independent language study.
aims: Develop English communication ability, emphasizing speaking and listening skills
goals: Develop English communication skills, including for speaking and presenting
Improve English pronunciation and listening ability
Learn English related to students' areas of interest
Attain skills for independent language study

plan.text: Introductions, level assessment, selection of course material

Topic to be decided
Students will study recorded audio/video content. The instructor will assist students in understanding vocabulary and grammar in context. Students will summarize the content along with their thoughts on the topic. For homework, they will make vocabulary lists of words they have learned, with original example sentences.
Students will present on the content studied in the previous lesson and engage in class discussion.

textbooks: The instructor will provide all necessary materials.
prereq: Students will be graded on homework, presentations, and tests. Good attendance is required in order to pass.
prep:
Students must complete homework and do other preparation for speaking activities, in-class presentations, and quizzes.
Students can expect approximately 30 minutes of homework for each class period.
teachers: ◎ ロバートソン マイケル
